objective: Grow revenue from the existing catalog by lifting average order value AND conversion rate, WITHOUT adding checkout friction and WITHOUT breaking multi-currency correctness or PCI compliance.
strategy: (1) grow basket size via complementary-gear bundling and recommendations; (2) improve product discovery and trust so browsers convert; (3) keep the funnel fast and reliable at seasonal peak.

entries.text:
Here's where I'd focus:

**Tactics I'd drive:**
1. Instrument the conversion funnel end-to-end across catalog → recommendations → cart → checkout so I can see where browsers drop off and where bundling actually lifts AOV — not guess at it.

entries.text: I agree with Elena, Sofia, and Priya that bundles, recommendations, and discovery surfaces are core to lifting AOV and conversion—but they need to be merchandising-defined against the actual catalog categories, not separately invented by ads or recommendations. entries.prompt: PRE-MORTEM. It is one year from now and this initiative failed badly. From YOUR role's vantage, tell the short story of what went wrong: the specific failure in or adjacent to your domain that caused
entries.text: **The story:** We announced the "trail-ready bundle" launch — boots + jacket + shirt — to leadership and turned on Sofia's ad spend for peak season. Jira said done across all five teams. But the currency validation had only passed in USD and EUR; the CAD and AUD bundle prices were rendering off by rounding, and the recommendations service wasn't actually deployed to the cart page yet — only the pr

entries.prompt: PRE-MORTEM. It is one year from now and this initiative failed badly. From YOUR role's vantage, tell the short story of what went wrong: the specific failure in or adjacent to your domain that caused
entries.text: A year from now, I’d say we failed because merchandising changes stayed trapped in engineering tickets while ads, recommendations, cart, and checkout each reflected slightly different versions of the outdoor-gear catalog. synthesis.text:
# Kickoff Synthesis — Bundling & Recommendations Peak-Season Initiative

**Status: SYNTHETIC, unratified panel input for human judgment. Real disagreements are preserved, not smoothed.**

Model-family key: claude = {product-manager, compliance-officer, finance-analyst, frontend-engineer}; gpt = {merchandising, sre, customer-support}; gemini = {payments, marketing, engineering-director}.

---

## Risk Register

| Risk | Flagged by | Corroboration |
|------|-----------|---------------|
| **"Done" is a Jira fiction** — features reported shipped but not actually deployed across all 5+ services | Elena (claude), Rachel (gemini), Sam (gpt) | **CROSS-FAMILY** |
| **Multi-currency price/rounding correctness** — FX rounding compounds across bundled line items in 6 currencies; wrong totals in UI | Elena (claude), Jordan (gpt), David (gemini), Daniel (claude), Priya (claude) | **CROSS-FAMILY** |
